mechanism_narrative: Low federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive forward returns for the S&P 500 over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 6.0 percent indicates deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending, which directly suppresses corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns remain positive in >70% of instances when UNRATE crosses above 6.0%, or signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales reflects synchronized domestic demand growth, which amplifies corporate revenue forecasts and produces positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter-ahead horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when GDPC1 and RSAFS move in opposite directions, or 60-day forward returns are negative in >60% of co-movement periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices signal strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which translates to higher corporate profit margins and positive equity returns over a one-month horizon during recovery phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to generate positive 20-day returns during post-shock recovery periods, or the signal's predictive accuracy drops below baseline (Rank IC < 0.02) in this specific regime.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index levels above 230 indicate accelerating inflationary pressures that force central banks to tighten policy, raising discount rates and triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL readings above 230 are followed by positive 5-day returns in >65% of cases, or the threshold effect vanishes in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects robust labor market expansion and rising household income, which sustains consumer spending and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows no statistical correlation with 60-day forward returns (Rank IC < 0.01) across multiple rolling windows, or positive payroll changes are followed by negative returns in >60% of periods.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent strength in housing starts and retail sales demonstrates synchronized consumer confidence and durable goods demand, which boosts corporate earnings projections and yields positive S&P 500 returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: The combined housing and retail signal fails to predict positive returns when HOUST exceeds 600.0, or 20-day forward returns are negative in >65% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production falling below 95.0 signals manufacturing contraction and supply-side bottlenecks, which compresses profit margins and generates negative S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days during late-cycle phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO readings below 95.0 are followed by positive 5-day returns in >70% of cases during late-cycle expansions, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02) in walk-forward validation.
